findings.title: get_debug_flag() now always returns a boolean, silently breaking callers that checked for None
findings.detail: Previously `get_debug_flag(default=None)` could return None to signal 'not set by environment'. Now it always returns True or False. The old `default` parameter was removed without deprecation. In `flask/cli.py` (line 357) and `flask/app.py` `make_config()` (line 644), there are `if debug is not None:` guards that were specifically designed to tolerate None. Now the guard is always True (since get_debug_flag() never returns None), meaning the debug flag will always be forced to False when FLASK_DEBUG is unset and FLASK_ENV is not 'development'—potentially overriding an application that sets `app.debug = True` programmatically before the CLI loads it.

findings.title: ScriptInfo.load_app() unconditionally overwrites app.debug because get_debug_flag() never returns None
findings.detail: The guard `if debug is not None` at line 361 is now always True because `get_debug_flag()` always returns a bool. This means the CLI always sets `app.debug` via the env var reading path, overriding any value set programmatically inside the app factory. Before this PR, if no FLASK_DEBUG env var was set and no FLASK_ENV was set, `get_debug_flag(default=None)` returned None, the guard was False, and the app's own debug setting was respected. This behavioral change is not documented.

findings.title: Dead code in test_get_debug_flag: the None branch can never be reached
findings.detail: After changing the parametrize table, no row has `expected_flag = None` anymore (the `('', None, True)` row became `('', False, False)`). The `if expected_flag is None: assert get_debug_flag() is None` branch at line 897-898 is now dead code that will never execute. It should be removed to keep tests honest and readable.

findings.title: debug property setter unnecessarily delegates to _set_debug_value
findings.detail: The `_set_debug` method (line 797-798) does nothing but call `_set_debug_value`. There is no reason to have two separate private methods for this. Either inline `_set_debug_value` into `_set_debug` or assign `_set_debug_value` directly as the setter. This adds dead indirection that makes the code harder to read.

findings.title: Default config values for ENV and DEBUG are None in default_config but are immediately overwritten
findings.detail: The `default_config` ImmutableDict lists `'ENV': None` and `'DEBUG': None`, but `make_config()` always overwrites these with `get_env()` and `get_debug_flag()` before returning the config. The None values in `default_config` are misleading—they falsely suggest these keys actually default to None. This could confuse subclasses that read `default_config` directly, or documentation generators. Consider not listing them in `default_config` at all, or documenting clearly that they are populated dynamically.

findings.title: get_env() treats empty string FLASK_ENV as 'production' without warning
findings.detail: The check `if not val` will treat an empty-string value of FLASK_ENV as unset and silently fall back to 'production'. Users who accidentally export FLASK_ENV='' will silently get 'production' with no warning or error. This is somewhat inconsistent since the test case `('', 'production', False)` in test_get_env confirms this behavior but it may surprise users. Prefer an explicit None check: `if val is None:` using `os.environ.get('FLASK_ENV', None)` to match the intent more precisely.

findings.title: test_get_env lacks coverage for FLASK_DEBUG overriding FLASK_ENV=development
findings.detail: There is no test case verifying that when both `FLASK_ENV=development` and `FLASK_DEBUG=0` are set, `get_debug_flag()` correctly returns False (FLASK_DEBUG wins). Similarly, there is no test for `FLASK_ENV=development` and `FLASK_DEBUG=1` together. The interaction between the two env vars is a critical invariant of the new design and should be tested.

findings.title: Env printed in run_command reads from env var directly, not from the loaded app's config
findings.detail: `get_env()` is called at line 631 to print the current environment before the app loads. If an application factory overrides `app.config['ENV']` (which is allowed since `ENV` is now a proper config key), the printed value will not reflect the actual app env. At minimum a comment explaining this should be added; better would be to print it after the app loads, or print a warning if the two values differ.

findings.title: Contradictory wording: 'enable all development features (and to disable the debug mode)'
findings.detail: The phrase 'To enable all development features (and to disable the debug mode)' is self-contradictory. Setting FLASK_ENV=development *enables* debug mode, it does not disable it. This is clearly an editing mistake. It should say something like 'To enable all development features (including debug mode)' or similar.

findings.title: RST code block not indented — shell commands will render as plain text
findings.detail: The example shell commands `$ export FLASK_ENV=development` and `$ flask run` are placed at column 0 after a `::` directive but are not indented. In reStructuredText a literal block introduced by `::` requires the block to be indented relative to the surrounding text. Without indentation these lines will be rendered as ordinary paragraph text, not as a formatted code example.
